data.title: Fed Gov. Christopher Waller emerges as likely successor to Jerome Powell as Fed chair. He advocated for 25-basis-point rate cut in July FOMC meeting, reflecting Trump's preference for aggressive rate cuts. Waller's track record and economic philosophy impress Trump team advisers.
data.description: According to sources tied to the Trump administration, Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller is now a top contender to succeed Jerome Powell as the next Fed chair. The GLD ETF has shown significant movement recently. This choice signals a rift within the Federal Reserve and Trump's preference for a more aggressive approach to cutting rates. Waller's dissent during the July FOMC meeting, where he pushed for an immediate 25-basis-point rate cut in contrast to Powell and the majority's decision to maintain rates, has garnered attention. Advisers close to Trump have been swayed by Waller's proactive stance on rate cuts and his strategy of basing policy decisions on forecasts rather than historical data. With a strong background in the Federal Reserve System, including positions at the St. Louis Fed and in academia, Waller's support is steadily growing. To read the complete article, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Si2 establishes LLM Benchmarking Coalition in Austin, Texas to advance large language models for semiconductor design.
data.description: In Austin, Texas, Si2 has revealed the establishment of the LLM benchmarking coalition with the aim to accelerate the advancement of superior large language models for semiconductor design.

data.title: Icertis CEO Samir Bodas transitions to Executive Chairman; COO Anand Subbaraman named CEO. Company approaching $350M in annual revenue.
data.description: Bellevue-based company Icertis, a prominent player in AI-driven contract intelligence solutions, recently revealed a significant change in leadership. Samir Bodas, the CEO and Co-founder, will transition to the role of Executive Chairman following over 15 years of guiding the company with a visionary approach. Taking over as the new Chief Executive Officer is Anand Subbaraman, who previously served as the Chief Operating Officer. Icertis, established in 2009 by Samir Bodas and Monish Darda, experienced remarkable growth under their leadership, achieving a substantial annual recurring revenue of nearly $350 million, solidifying its position as a leading force in the industry.

data.title: SoftBank reported Q1 net sales of 1.82 trillion JPY ($12.54B), up from 1.70 trillion JPY ($11.71B) a year ago. Arm sales rose 4% to 152.9 billion JPY ($1.05B), with a divisional loss of 8.7 billion JPY ($60M) due to increased R&D expenses.
data.description: SoftBank Group announced its first-quarter results, revealing a significant increase in quarterly net sales to 1.82 trillion Japanese yen ($12.54 billion) from 1.70 trillion yen ($11.71 billion) in the previous year. The company's net sales for the SoftBank segment grew by 8% to 1.66 trillion yen ($11.43 billion), while Arm saw a 4% increase in net sales to 152.9 billion yen ($1.05 billion). The division faced a loss of 8.7 billion yen ($60 million) compared to a profit of 10.2 billion yen in the prior year due to higher research and development expenses. This news suggests potential shifts in investment strategies for SoftBank.

data.title: Luxury goods market projected to reach $704.95 billion by 2033 from $382.8 billion in 2024, with a 7.02% CAGR growth.
data.description: A report titled "Luxury Goods Market Overview and Forecast 2025-2033" has recently been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The Luxury Goods Market is projected to grow significantly, reaching US$704.95 billion by 2033, up from US$382.8 billion in 2024. The industry is expected to experience a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 7.02% from 2025 to 2033. Several factors are driving this growth, including rising disposable incomes, a growing middle-class consumer base, increased brand recognition, digital transformations, expansion into emerging markets, a preference for experiential luxury, influencer marketing, and a focus on sustainability.
